METALWORK PLANT JSC — Annual Efficiency Optimization Report

Project: Production Line Modernization — Phase II

Equipment Upgrade:
The plant has completed a major equipment upgrade for the hot rolling
workshop. New electric arc furnaces replaced aging Soviet-era units.
Energy consumption reduced by 14% compared to the previous year.
Industrial gas consumption: 2.4 million m³ (natural gas for furnaces).

Waste Management:
Production scrap recycling rate improved from 53% to 67%.
Industrial wastewater treatment follows standard municipal discharge
norms. No specialized environmental treatment system installed.

Production:
Primary product: rolled steel profiles for the construction sector.
Annual output: 85,000 tonnes.
Total workforce: 1,240 employees.

Energy Supply:
No renewable energy systems installed or planned at this time.
Facility operates entirely on national grid electricity.
Feasibility study recommends conventional grid capacity expansion.

Certification:
No environmental management certification obtained or planned.
Quality management: ISO 9001 recertification in progress.

Regulatory:
All industrial discharge permits current. No violations on record.
